title: rubus procumbens
intro: Rubus procumbens, also known as the creeping raspberry, is a perennial plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties due to the presence of salicylic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Rubus procumbens include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rubus procumbens can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
